title: arum orientale subsp. elongatum
intro: Arum orientale subsp. elongatum, also known as the Giant Dragon Arum or Dracunculus vulcanicola, is a plant species within the Arum genus, characterized by its large, green, umbrella-like spathe and white spadix flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation and pain, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional remedies. Herbal preparations of Arum orientale subsp. elongatum can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and creams, often used topically to treat various skin conditions and ailments.
